rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGH ELWOOD MACBETH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner notes. a. Please explain why the petition alleges Decedent died September 12, 2019. The petition for probate and Order for Probate state the Decedent died September 14, 2019. b. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.35]. c. The I&A alleges under #4 by order of the court dated (specify) 12/23/2019 [No Sch 2 Items]. Please explain the order of the court dated 12/23/2019 appointed Karen Ann Simons as referee. d. There is no attached detailed schedules of receipts. 1 total amount for all the receipts is not detailed and must be amended. When an account has been waived, the report must contain the information required by rule 7.550. If the report is accompanied by a request for statutory commissions or fees, the basis for their computation must be included in the petition substantially in the form provided in (a). Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705]. e. Please explain why the calculation of statutory fees allege $102.10 in additional interest accrued when the account alleges $90.86 in interest. f. Please explain the calculation of statutory fees. The Final I&A alleges $318,591.62. The account alleges $90.86 in interest. However, the calculation alleges $102.10 in interest. Please explain how $319,693.72 is the basis for the calculations of statutory compensation. g. The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] h. Please explain who High E. Macbeth III is and why High E. Macbeth III listed as an heir of Decedent on Exhibit C and person entitled to notice on Exhibit D. i. The prayer on page 11 of the petition does not explain how the estate is to be distributed. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. j. The proposed order must match what is requested in the petition. The proposed order page 5 #4 is not in the prayer of the petition.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RALPH R. OROQUITA REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene And Expunge Notices Of Pending Action (Lis Pendens)
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Settlor Ralph R. Oroquita, Sr., created the Ralph R. Oroquita Revocable Trust ("Trust") on September 29, 2016, naming himself and his son Petitioner Ralph R. Oroquita, Jr. ("Oroquita") as cotrustees. The Settlor listed the real property located at 460 Peninsula Avenue, San Francisco, CA (the "Property") as a trust asset. On July 15, 2020, the Settlor executed the 2020 Amendment and Restatement of the Ralph R. Oroquita Revocable Trust ("Amendment") naming himself and his great-nephew Respondent Anthony A.J. McGurk ("McGurk") as cotrustees and removing Oroquita as a cotrustee. The Settlor again listed the Property as a trust asset. On July 20, 2021, Oroquita filed a Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Amendment and Damages & Attorney Fees pursuant to Probate Code Section 17200 ("Trust Contest") alleging that the settlor did not have capacity to execute the Amendment, and further alleging undue influence, financial elder abuse, and intentional interference with inheritance against McGurk. McGurk denies the allegations. On July 21, 2021, Oroquita recorded a Notice of Pending Action, and on August 2, 2021, Oroquita recorded an Amended Notice of Pending Action. On August 23, 2021, escrow closed and a Grant Deed transferring ownership of the Property from McGurk, as trustee, to Ruan and Kuang was recorded. Now before the court is Ruan and Kuang's Motion to Intervene and Expunge Notices of Pending Action. Ruan and Kuang's unopposed request for judicial notice is GRANTED. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Ruan and Kuang's unopposed leave to intervene is GRANTED. While it is true that Nonparties must seek leave to intervene before they may file a motion to expunge pursuant to Section CCP 405.30, and in this case, they sought leave to intervene in their notice of motion and motion, as the current property owners they have an interest in the property and standing to bring this motion. Since, no objection has been raised to the failure to seek leave to intervene before filing the motion, the infirmities are excusable and, in any event, Oroquita has not demonstrated prejudice and no substantial prejudice is apparent on this record.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RALPH R. OROQUITA REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene And Expunge Notices Of Pending Action (Lis Pendens) (Added For The Purpose Of Posting The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [3 of 3] Ruan and Kuang's sanctions request is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Under CCP section 405.38, the court is required to impose a reasonable monetary sanction against the losing party unless it finds the losing party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. Here, prevailing parties seek sanctions in the amount of 36 hours of attorneys' fees, 28 hours for researching and preparing the motion and 8 hours for preparing for preparing the reply and attending the hearing. Much of the briefing is related to the argument that Ruan and Kuang did not have actual knowledge of the lis pendens, which is irrelevant to this motion, as well as to whether Probate Code Section 18100 applies, which is also irrelevant to this motion. Moreover, the court has found that Oroquita has pleaded a real property claim contrary to the argument presented by Ruan and Kuang, but that he has not established the probable validity of his claim in this motion. Under these circumstances, a sanctions award of 10 hours of attorney time is a reasonable sanction, and therefore $3,650.00 in fees and $60 in costs is awarded to Ruan and Kuang. That sum is to be paid within 10 days of the filing of this order. Ruan and Kuang shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RALPH R. OROQUITA REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene And Expunge Notices Of Pending Action (Lis Pendens) (Added For The Purpose Of Posting The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 3] Ruan and Kuang's Motion to Expunge Notices of Pending Action is GRANTED. Unlike other motions, when a motion to expunge is brought, the burden is on the party opposing the motion to show the existence of a real property claim and to establish the probable validity of that claim by a preponderance of the evidence. CCP Sections 405.31 and 405.32. The question of whether a pleading states a real property claim is tested by a "demurrer-like analysis" that centers on the adequacy of the pleadings. Kirkeby v. Sup. Ct. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 642, 648. It is strictly a binary process: If you properly plead a real property claim, you can file a notice of lis pendens; if you do not, you cannot. See Ibid. The pleadings in this case include a copy of the 2016 Trust and the 2020 Amendment. Both trust documents (at page 19 of the 2016 Trust and page 18 of the 2020 Amendment) specifically list as an asset of the Trust: "The real property located at 460 Peninsula Avenue, San Francisco, CA together with any insurance on the property and all of the contents located therein or on the property." Furthermore, the Trust named Oroquita as trustee and the Amendment named McGurk as trustee. If the cause of action (trust contest as to the Amendment) is meritorious, the title to the Property will be affected. That is, if the trust contest is successful and the Amendment is found invalid, legal title to the Property will be held by Oroquita (the trustee of the 2016 Trust) not McGurk (the trustee of the 2020 Amendment). Oroquita has pleaded a real property claim. The burden is on Oroquita to establish the probable validity of his real property claim by a preponderance of the evidence to avoid a motion to expunge. CCP Section 405.32. Probable validity means it is more likely than not that Oroquita will obtain a judgment against McGurk on his petition. CCP Section 405.3. Oroquita may offer evidence, and the court will weigh conflicting evidence in deciding if Oroquita sustained his burden. Oroquita has presented a declaration as evidence; however, even taking all the facts in Oroquita's declaration as true, it is insufficient evidence to support a decision that Oroquita is more likely than not to prevail on his claims. Expungement of the improper lis pendens is mandatory since the court finds that the underlying claim's probable validity has not been established by a preponderance of the evidence.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAN OEHL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/12/22 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: In a supplement to the amended petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, the information required by item 5a(2) of the petition must be answered. Copies of the bank statements immediately preceding the date of death must be attached to the supplement. A guardian ad litem should be appointed to represent the minor's interests. This may be done via an ex parte petition. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEITH MICHAEL MORSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/2021. 1) Continued to allow time to process e-filings. 2) Please explain why this probate is necessary when the estate is under $166,250.00? (See Probate Code 13100 et seq.) 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/6/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STEPHEN DON WORLDIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Court Order Determining Succession To Personal Property And For Treble Damages
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/04/2022 (no earlier date available), as the following issues e-mailed to counsel for the petitioner on 10/27/2021 remain unaddressed: 1) No personal representative of the decedent's estate appears to have been appointed. Does the petitioner have standing to bring this petition in the probate department of this Court? See Probate Code section 13111(d). 2) Did the decedent leave real property of small value? If not, please explain why sections 13150 et seq. should apply. 3) Please provide the balance of the Union Bank account as of the time Carol Berman presented the small estate affidavit to the bank. 4) Please describe Carol Berman's relationship to the decedent. 5) Please provide a branch location of the bank where the small estate affidavit was presented. 6) Please provide a branch location of the bank where the decedent's account was maintained. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. petitioner may be asked to clarify the changes in the amended petition. one appears to be the first name of the father and possibly additional attachments. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
